quote_of_day.text:
Man fuck that guy
 do ur moral policing in jewtube or goystagram.
If u want to censor this board then what's the use. Are u new to IB culture we tolerate evrything except illegal things.
If it's not illegal it's ok.(With caveats)
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of sexual frustration moved over the board, bringing scattered showers of pornography discussion. Anon engaged in a lengthy drizzle of comments on pornhwa and moral policing.

A brief but intense heat wave of male gaze sparked controversy, as one anon praised curvy women as symbols of fertility. This sparked a flurry of responses but eventually subsided.

Fog rolled in over several threads, with anon expressing despair over personal circumstances, including height. Online claims of harm targeting foreign men also circulated, though details were unclear.

articles.title: Inchads discuss pornhwa and moral policing
articles.body:
Anon started a thread on July 2, 2026, asking if anyone else reads pornhwa.

One respondent expressed frustration with what they perceived as moral policing on the board, telling another anon to take their concerns to YouTube or Instagram if they wanted to censor content.

Another anon argued that people who commit harmful acts do so because they don't care about harming others, and that they don't need to be provoked.

A different poster claimed that some users on the board derail threads with their opinions, and called for the moderators to 'pull the plug' on such discussions.

One user referenced a post saying that real children are 'gross and cringe' like everything 3D, and that people prefer loli because they like flat chests and small heights.

Anon also brought up the topic of smut fiction, questioning whether it would be considered bestiality if it involved intercourse between beasts and humans.

Some users debated whether it's okay to consume certain types of content, with one saying that they read crime and punishment and changed themselves for the better.

A respondent criticized what they saw as hypocrisy, pointing out that some users condemn certain types of content while tolerating others. Anon also joked about adding IQ puzzles as a CAPTCHA, saying that some users can't differentiate fiction from reality.

The discussion also touched on the topic of loli porn, with one user expressing a desire for it and another calling them 'sick-minded'. One poster argued that pornhwa has better storytelling than some movies and most porn videos.

The conversation was marked by disagreements over free speech and the limits of acceptable discussion on the board. One user asserted their right to express their views, saying that this is a freedom of speech image board.

articles.title: Anon Praises 'Beautiful Curvy Women' as Symbols of Fertility
articles.body:
Anon expressed admiration for 'beautiful curvy women' as symbols of fertility and continuity of human progeny.

They mentioned that women like these supposedly roamed the Gupta era, a strong kingdom that actively destroyed the 'mleccha dasyu whites' and laid the foundation for the numeric system, mathematics, and architecture.

Another anon responded with a bleak outlook, stating they were 'blackpilled' for India's future. They claimed that women, by biology, cannot work as effectively as men for 30 days and questioned the economic value of women if office jobs are removed.

A respondent argued that ancient Indian temples were practically banks and it was inevitable that they would be razed for economic gain or to sustain war expeditions.

They also suggested that the OP had a '2-dimensional view' and might be a 'naarthie'. Anon also discussed the relationship between genetics and physical appearance.

One claimed that the Y chromosome is unchanging and that its nature is not to mix or change its core component.

Another anon countered, stating that Y chromosome SRY cannot change and that 'steppe haplo Y' has not changed since it inherently lacks the ability to splice like the X chromosome.

The conversation also touched on the topic of race and interracial relationships. Beefanon suggested that instead of trying to divide people based on sex, there should be a focus on improving the human race as a whole.

They also argued that a mixed-race kid is less genetically related to an individual than an unrelated Indian kid, making them a 'literal cuck' if they engage in race-mixing. The discussion further devolved into topics of genetics, diet, and personal appearance.

Anon and Beefanon exchanged views on the perceived physical and genetic superiority of certain groups, with some using derogatory terms to describe others.

articles.title: Anon sparks controversy with LGBT comment
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread with a simple 'Should i'. One anon responded with 'Pajeet LGBT community is full of uggo chamars. Only reason i don't wanna come out, imagine being associated with the retards.' This comment sparked a series of replies.

Another anon chimed in with 'Yes suck musal cocks. Cut cocks are better to suck. No dirty foreskins.' A respondent shot back with '>>33337 Fuck off pasmande slumdwelling zuttslut'.

The conversation took a turn with '>>33337 hard to wrap your lips around a microscopic object'. Anon also joked about brahmin colour, saying '>>33334 which is brahmin in colour' and '>>33336 Brahmin pink'.

The thread also included several brief interjections, including '>>33325 Become', '>>33330 Sissy', '>>33329 Lil', '>>33328 A', and a single question: 'nigger?'.

articles.title: Anon claims gobarjeetas discuss harming foreign men
articles.body:
Anon posted about online spaces where thousands of gobarjeetas allegedly discuss harming foreign men and blaming Indian men.

Another anon replied that gobars suffer more racism than Indian men, citing a video of someone named Timmy Trash.

A respondent claimed hate spread by gobarjeetas against Indian men results in racism against India and affects Indian women. Anon described gobars as lacking their own thinking and being manipulated by media and 'mullahwood'.

articles.title: Anon expresses despair over height
articles.body:
Anon posted 'No wonder I am who i am
it is more than over for me'.

Anon replied to an unseen post saying some short girls exist but one needs to be at least 175cm to have a chance with them. Another Anon said anyone shorter than 180cm should 'just rope'.

One respondent empathized, saying 'It's owari da for manlets
World is too cruel against manlets'. Anon asked about someone's height, getting a reply of 161cm.
